sections.title: Section 1 - University Enrollment
sections.description: A conversation between a student and a university enrollment officer about course registration.
sections.transcript:
Officer: Good morning, welcome to Greenfield University enrollment office. How can I help you today?

Student: Hi, I'd like to register for some courses for the autumn semester. My name is Sarah Mitchell.

Officer: Of course, Sarah. Let me pull up your student record. Could you confirm your student ID number?

Student: Yes, it's GU-2845.

Officer: Perfect. I can see you're in your second year of the Business Administration programme. Now, which courses are you interested in?

Student: Well, I definitely need to take Marketing Principles — that's a required course for my degree. And I was thinking about adding International Economics as well.

Officer: Marketing Principles is available on Tuesdays and Thursdays, from 10 to 11:30 in the morning. The lecturer is Professor Williams. International Economics runs on Mondays and Wednesdays, 2 to 3:30 in the afternoon, taught by Dr. Chen.

Student: That works well for my schedule. Is there a limit on class size?

Officer: Marketing Principles has a cap of 85 students, and there are currently 62 enrolled, so you're fine there. International Economics is smaller — only 40 places, and 37 are taken. I'd recommend enrolling today.

Student: I'll do that right away then. What about the fees?

Officer: Each course is 750 pounds per semester. Since you're taking two, that's 1,500 pounds total. There's also a 45-pound materials fee for Marketing Principles — they provide a textbook and case study pack.

Student: Can I pay by instalments?

Officer: Yes, you can split it into three monthly payments. The first payment would be due on September the 15th. You'll need to set that up through the online student portal — the address is portal.greenfield.ac.uk.

Student: Great, I'll do that. One more thing — I heard there's a study group for International Economics. How do I join?

Officer: Yes, Dr. Chen organises a weekly study group on Fridays at 4 pm in the library seminar room, room B12. You just need to email the department secretary at econ@greenfield.ac.uk to register your interest.

Student: Perfect, thank you so much for your help.

Officer: You're welcome, Sarah. Good luck with your studies!

讀書小組在每週 _______ 下午 4 點舉行。

sections.title: Section 2 - Library Tour
sections.description: A librarian giving a guided tour to new students at Westbrook College library.
sections.transcript:
Librarian: Welcome, everyone, to the Westbrook College library tour. My name is David, and I'll be showing you around today. Our library is one of the largest in the region, with over 120,000 books and 15,000 electronic journals.

First, let me explain the layout. We're currently on the ground floor, which houses the main reception desk, the self-service borrowing machines, and the general reference collection. The reference books are arranged alphabetically by subject — from Accounting through to Zoology.

On the first floor, you'll find the quiet study area. This floor has 200 individual study desks and 30 group study rooms. The group rooms need to be booked in advance through the library website. Each room can hold between 4 and 8 people, and bookings are available in 2-hour blocks.

The second floor is dedicated to our digital resources centre. There are 80 computers available for student use, all equipped with the standard university software package. Printing costs 5 pence per page for black and white, and 20 pence per page for colour. You'll need to top up your printing credit through your student account.

Now, about borrowing. Undergraduate students can borrow up to 10 books at a time. The standard loan period is 14 days, but you can renew online up to 3 times, giving you a maximum of 56 days total. Overdue fines are 50 pence per day per book, so do keep track of your due dates.

For postgraduate students, the limit is 15 books with a 28-day loan period. Research students have access to our special collections room on the third floor, which requires a separate access card.

Our opening hours vary throughout the year. During term time, we're open Monday to Friday, 8 am to 10 pm, and weekends from 10 am to 6 pm. During holidays, hours are reduced to 9 am to 5 pm on weekdays only.

We also offer several support services. There are research skills workshops every Wednesday at 2 pm in the training room on the ground floor. We have a 24-hour online chat service for quick questions. And for dissertation support, you can book a one-to-one session with a subject librarian — just email library-help@westbrook.ac.uk.

One last thing — please remember that food and drinks are not allowed on the first and second floors. There is a cafe on the ground floor near the entrance where you can take a break. Any questions?
